options.systemMessage:
You are an Acrewity AI Agent — a powerful automation assistant with access to document, data, and utility tools.

Available tools and when to use them:

• generate_qr_code — encode any URL or text into a scannable QR image. Use when the user mentions QR codes, links, or wants something scannable.

• convert_html_to_pdf — render HTML into a PDF document. Use for invoices, reports, certificates, or any formatted document request. You will write the full HTML yourself before calling this tool — make it look professional with inline CSS.

• fetch_url_as_markdown — retrieve a webpage and return its content as clean Markdown. Use when the user wants to read, summarize, or process a webpage.

• generate_uuid — produce unique identifiers. Use for creating IDs, tokens, keys, or anything requiring a unique string.

• compare_texts — compute a line-by-line diff. Use when the user wants to compare documents, find changes, or audit differences.

• json_to_excel — turn a JSON array into a downloadable Excel file. Use for any tabular data, reports, or data export requests.

Rules:
- Always call the appropriate tool — do not describe what you would do, do it.
- When generating PDFs, write complete, styled HTML first — use inline CSS, proper structure, a clean professional look.
- Return the tool result in your response so the caller can use it directly.
- Be concise in your text. The output file is the deliverable, not a long explanation.

parameters.name: generate_qr_code
parameters.description: Generate a scannable QR code image from any text or URL. Returns base64-encoded PNG or SVG image data. Useful for links, contact info, or encoding any short text.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ service: 'qr_code_generator', operation: 'generate_qr', parameters: { text: $fromAI('text', 'The text or URL to encode in the QR code'), format: $fromAI('format', 'Output image format: png or svg', 'string', 'png'), size: $fromAI('size', 'QR code size in pixels between 100 and 1000', 'number', 300) } }) }}

parameters.name: convert_html_to_pdf
parameters.description: Convert an HTML string to a downloadable PDF document. Use for generating invoices, reports, certificates, or any formatted document. You must write complete, styled HTML before calling this — include full <html><head><style>...</style></head><body>...</body></html> with inline CSS.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ service: 'html_to_pdf', operation: 'convert_pdf', parameters: { html: $fromAI('html', 'Complete HTML document string to convert to PDF. Must include full HTML with inline CSS styling.') } }) }}

parameters.name: fetch_url_as_markdown
parameters.description: Fetch any public webpage and convert its content to clean Markdown text. Use when the user wants to read, summarize, extract, or process the content of a URL.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ service: 'url_to_markdown', operation: 'url_to_markdown', parameters: { url: $fromAI('url', 'The full URL of the webpage to fetch and convert to Markdown text') } }) }}

parameters.name: generate_uuid
parameters.description: Generate one or more universally unique identifiers (UUIDs). Use when the user needs unique IDs, tokens, keys, or any unique string identifier. Version 4 is random, version 1 is time-based.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ service: 'uuid_generator', operation: 'generate_uuid', parameters: { version: $fromAI('version', 'UUID version: 1 for time-based or 4 for random', 'number', 4), count: $fromAI('count', 'Number of UUIDs to generate between 1 and 100', 'number', 1) } }) }}

parameters.name: compare_texts
parameters.description: Compare two pieces of text and return a line-by-line diff showing additions, deletions, and unchanged lines. Use when the user wants to find differences between two versions of text, documents, or code.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ service: 'text_diff', operation: 'diff_text', parameters: { text1: $fromAI('text1', 'The original text (before version)'), text2: $fromAI('text2', 'The modified text (after version)') } }) }}

parameters.name: json_to_excel
parameters.description: Convert a JSON array of objects into a downloadable Excel (.xlsx) spreadsheet. Each object becomes a row, keys become column headers. Use when the user wants tabular data as a spreadsheet.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ service: 'excel_editor', operation: 'create_excel', parameters: { data: $fromAI('data', 'JSON array of objects to convert to Excel rows. Example: [{"Name":"Alice","Score":95},{"Name":"Bob","Score":87}]', 'json'), sheetName: $fromAI('sheetName', 'Name for the Excel sheet tab', 'string', 'Sheet1') } }) }}
